  "text": "## Configuration Documentation Pattern in Apache Kafka\n\n### Pattern Overview\nApache Kafka uses a class-based configuration pattern (not annotation-based) for defining and documenting configuration properties. While the task refers to @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef 'annotations', these do not exist as Java annotations in the Kafka codebase. Instead, Kafka uses:\n\n1. **ConfigDef class** (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java): A class for specifying the set of expected configurations, with detailed metadata for each configuration key including name, type, default value, documentation, validator, importance level, group information, and dependency information.\n\n2. **ConfigKey inner class** (defined within ConfigDef.java, line 1237): A static inner class within ConfigDef that represents individual configuration keys with comprehensive metadata.\n\n3. **AbstractConfig class** (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java): Base class extended by configuration classes throughout Kafka that holds parsed and original configuration values.\n\n### Configuration Documentation Generators\nKafka provides documentation generation tools that extract configuration information at runtime:\n\n- **PredicateDoc** (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java): Generates HTML documentation for Kafka Connect predicates by reflecting on OVERVIEW_DOC and CONFIG_DEF static fields.\n\n- **TransformationDoc** (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java): Generates HTML documentation for Kafka Connect transformations (Cast, DropHeaders, ExtractField, Filter, Flatten, HeaderFrom, HoistField, InsertField, InsertHeader, MaskField, RegexRouter, ReplaceField, SetSchemaMetadata, TimestampConverter, TimestampRouter, ValueToKey).\n\n### AbstractConfig Subclasses\nThroughout the Kafka codebase, 53+ classes extend AbstractConfig and define CONFIG_DEF static fields for configuration management. Key categories include:\n\n**Client-side configurations:**\n- AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n\n**Kafka Connect configurations:**\n- ConverterConfig, SimpleHeaderConverter, MirrorClientConfig, ConnectorConfig, SinkConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, RestServerConfig\n- Converter implementations: ByteArrayConverter, BooleanConverter, NumberConverter\n- Transform implementations: Cast, DropHeaders, ExtractField, Filter, Flatten, HeaderFrom, HoistField, InsertField, InsertHeader, MaskField, RegexRouter, ReplaceField, SetSchemaMetadata, TimestampConverter, TimestampRouter, ValueToKey\n- Predicate implementations: HasHeaderKey, RecordIsTombstone, TopicNameMatches\n\n**Server-side configurations:**\n- AbstractKafkaConfig, ServerConfigs, QuotaConfig, SocketServerConfigs, DelegationTokenManagerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs, ClientMetricsConfigs, MetricConfigs, BrokerSecurityConfigs\n\n**Component-specific configurations:**\n- GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareGroupConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig (coordinators)\n- MetadataLogConfig, QuorumConfig (raft)\n- LogConfig, CleanerConfig (storage)\n- AddPartitionsToTxnConfig, TransactionLogConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig (transaction coordinator)\n- StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig (streams)\n\nThis pattern provides a declarative, strongly-typed approach to configuration management without requiring Java annotations, using reflection and static field discovery for documentation generation."

  "text": "## Configuration Documentation Pattern in Apache Kafka\n\n### Pattern Overview\nApache Kafka uses a class-based configuration pattern (not annotation-based) for defining and documenting configuration properties. Key categories include:\n\n**Client-side configurations:**\n- AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n\n**Kafka Connect configurations:**\n- ConverterConfig, SimpleHeaderConverter, MirrorClientConfig, ConnectorConfig, SinkConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, RestServerConfig\n- Converter implementations: ByteArrayConverter, BooleanConverter, NumberConverter\n- Transform implementations: Cast, DropHeaders, ExtractField, Filter, Flatten, HeaderFrom, HoistField, InsertField, InsertHeader, MaskField, RegexRouter, ReplaceField, SetSchemaMetadata, TimestampConverter, TimestampRouter, ValueToKey\n- Predicate implementations: HasHeaderKey, RecordIsTombstone, TopicNameMatches\n\n**Server-side configurations:**\n- AbstractKafkaConfig, ServerConfigs, QuotaConfig, SocketServerConfigs, DelegationTokenManagerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs, ClientMetricsConfigs, MetricConfigs, BrokerSecurityConfigs\n\n**Component-specific configurations:**\n- GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareGroupConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig (coordinators)\n- MetadataLogConfig, QuorumConfig (raft)\n- LogConfig, CleanerConfig (storage)\n- AddPartitionsToTxnConfig, TransactionLogConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig (transaction coordinator)\n- StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig (streams)\n\nThis pattern provides a declarative, strongly-typed approach to configuration management without requiring Java annotations, using reflection and static field discovery for documentation generation."

  "text": "## Configuration Documentation Pattern in Apache Kafka\n\n### Pattern Overview\nApache Kafka uses a class-based configuration pattern (not annotation-based) for defining and documenting configuration properties. While the task refers to @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef 'annotations', these do not exist as Java annotations in the Kafka codebase. Instead, Kafka uses:\n\n1. **ConfigDef class** (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java): A class for specifying the set of expected configurations, with detailed metadata for each configuration key including name, type, default value, documentation, validator, importance level, group information, and dependency information.\n\n2. **ConfigKey inner class** (defined within ConfigDef.java, line 1237): A static inner class within ConfigDef that represents individual configuration keys with comprehensive metadata.\n\n3. **AbstractConfig class** (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java): Base class extended by configuration classes throughout Kafka that holds parsed and original configuration values.\n\n### Configuration Documentation Generators\nKafka provides documentation generation tools that extract configuration information at runtime:\n\n- **PredicateDoc** (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java): Generates HTML documentation for Kafka Connect predicates by reflecting on OVERVIEW_DOC and CONFIG_DEF static fields.\n\n- **TransformationDoc** (connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java): Generates HTML documentation for Kafka Connect transformations (Cast, DropHeaders, ExtractField, Filter, Flatten, HeaderFrom, HoistField, InsertField, InsertHeader, MaskField, RegexRouter, ReplaceField, SetSchemaMetadata, TimestampConverter, TimestampRouter, ValueToKey).\n\n### AbstractConfig Subclasses\nThroughout the Kafka codebase, 53+ classes extend AbstractConfig and define CONFIG_DEF static fields for configuration management. Key categories include:\n\n**Client-side configurations:**\n- AdminClientConfig, ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig\n\n**Kafka Connect configurations:**\n- ConverterConfig, SimpleHeaderConverter, MirrorClientConfig, ConnectorConfig, SinkConnectorConfig, TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, RestServerConfig\n- Converter implementations: ByteArrayConverter, BooleanConverter, NumberConverter\n- Transform implementations: Cast, DropHeaders, ExtractField, Filter, Flatten, HeaderFrom, HoistField, InsertField, InsertHeader, MaskField, RegexRouter, ReplaceField, SetSchemaMetadata, TimestampConverter, TimestampRouter, ValueToKey\n- Predicate implementations: HasHeaderKey, RecordIsTombstone, TopicNameMatches\n\n**Server-side configurations:**\n- AbstractKafkaConfig, ServerConfigs, QuotaConfig, SocketServerConfigs, DelegationTokenManagerConfigs, KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs, ClientMetricsConfigs, MetricConfigs, BrokerSecurityConfigs\n\n**Component-specific configurations:**\n- GroupCoordinatorConfig, ShareGroupConfig, ShareCoordinatorConfig (coordinators)\n- MetadataLogConfig, QuorumConfig (raft)\n- LogConfig, CleanerConfig (storage)\n- AddPartitionsToTxnConfig, TransactionLogConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig (transaction coordinator)\n- StreamsConfig, TopologyConfig (streams)\n\nThis pattern provides a declarative, strongly-typed approach to configuration management without requiring Java annotations, using reflection and static field discovery for documentation generation."
}
